title: Black Bear Pursuit - Resident
season: Spring: Mar 28-May 25, 2026 | Summer: July 5-July 31, 2026 | Fall: Nov 2-8, 2026
NOTES: Black Bear pursuit permit - resident: Pursuit only; this permit authorizes pursuit, but not harvest, of black bears. Season dates shown in the BR1007 print packet are Spring: Mar 28-May 25, 2026; Summer: July 5-July 31, 2026; Fall: Nov 2-8, 2026. The packet states a pursuit permit allows pursuit during open pursuit seasons and includes a warning that National Forests may contain private land requiring written permission. Full BR1007 area-specific pursuit information is indexed in 2026_black_bear_pursuit_BR1007_area_info_index.csv and sourced from black bear pursuit.pdf.
permit_note: Utah DWR Hunt Planner black bear pursuit export confirms resident pursuit hunt; no quota or permit total is published for pursuit-only permit.

title: Black Bear - Statewide Permit
NOTES: Black Bear statewide permit: Any open season on any open bear unit during the 2026 season. See the 2026 Black Bear Guidebook and the BR1000 print packet for season dates, legal methods, access notes, land ownership, and unit-specific restrictions. Special provisions: this packet identifies the statewide permit as a late-season bear conservation permit and includes the required harvest reporting language. Any harvested bear must be checked by a DWR employee within 48 hours of kill; permanent possession tag requirements apply. Full BR1000 area-specific information is indexed in 2026_black_bear_statewide_BR1000_area_info_index.csv and sourced from BR1000 Print.pdf.
permit_source_authority: Utah DWR Conservation Permit Working List
permit_note: Total count applied from Utah DWR 2025-2027 Multi-Year Conservation Permit working list. Resident/nonresident split is not published for these conservation permits.

title: Black Bear Harvest Objective Units
NOTES: Harvest-objective permits: Biologists set harvest objectives/quotas for certain bear units. When the objective is met, the unit closes to bear hunting for the rest of the season. A harvest-objective permit allows an eligible hunter to use any legal weapon to harvest one black bear on any open harvest-objective unit until the season ends or that unit objective is met. The permit is available to residents and nonresidents beginning March 10, 2026 at 8 a.m. MDT through wildlife.utah.gov, license agents, or DWR offices. A harvest-objective permit purchased after the season opens is not valid until three days from purchase; the last day to purchase a 2026 harvest-objective permit is Nov. 5, 2026. Hunters may not use bait with a harvest-objective permit. Fall harvest-objective hunts may include additional method restrictions such as no dogs/no bait or no bait, as listed by unit. Harvest-objective permits are available to eligible hunters except those who drew a bear limited-entry permit, not including pursuit or restricted pursuit permits. Hunters may only obtain one permit to harvest a bear during the 2026 season. Before every hunting trip, hunters must verify the unit is still open at wildlife.utah.gov/bearharvest. DWR updates closures daily by noon, effective the following day 30 minutes before official sunrise. After a harvest-objective unit closes, the permit may still be used on other open harvest-objective units. Multiseason limited-entry permit holders may hunt open harvest-objective seasons on the unit they draw without obtaining an additional harvest-objective permit; harvest-objective closures still apply. Source: 2026 Bear Cougar Furbearer Guidebook, Utah Admin. Rule R657-33-29 through R657-33-31. title: Black Bear Pursuit - Nonresident
season: Spring: Mar 28-May 25, 2026 | Summer: July 5-July 31, 2026 | Fall: Nov 2-8, 2026
NOTES: Black Bear pursuit permit - nonresident: Pursuit only; a pursuit permit authorizes pursuit, but not harvest, of black bears on units not designated as restricted during open pursuit seasons. Season dates are Spring: Mar 28-May 25, 2026; Summer: July 5-July 31, 2026; Fall: Nov 2-8, 2026. Nonresidents with pursuit permits may not use them on restricted pursuit units unless accompanying someone who possesses a valid restricted pursuit permit or hunting permit for that unit and season. See the 2026 Bear Cougar Furbearer Guidebook and hunt.utah.gov for complete unit availability.
